name: Pursuit
description_short: Pursuit is an SBA-approved lender offering small business loans in NYC, including SBA 504, 7(a), microloans, and proprietary products for working capital and real estate financing.
description_long:
Pursuit has been serving NYC-based entrepreneurs for nearly 70 years, establishing itself as a locally-focused business lender with deep understanding of the New York City market across all five boroughs. The company is approved under the SBA's Preferred Lenders Program and SBA Express Program, enabling it to originate federal small business loans with streamlined underwriting processes. Pursuit offers a comprehensive suite of financing products ranging from SBA-backed programs to proprietary offerings, addressing diverse business needs from startup capital to commercial real estate acquisition and refinancing.

Pursuit's product portfolio includes SBA 504 loans (up to $5.5M for owner-occupied commercial real estate), SBA 7(a) loans (up to $5M for working capital and general business needs), SBA Microloans (up to $50K), and several proprietary products including SmartLoan (up to $100K), small business lines of credit, and specialized programs like Veteran Loan Programs and Impact Loans (up to $500K). The company also administers state programs such as the New York Forward Loan Fund 2. Each product carries different qualification requirements and terms, with SBA 504 loans offering terms up to 25 years at below-market fixed rates.

What distinguishes Pursuit is its local expertise and multi-program approach. The company positions itself as an "ally" and "advocate" for NYC business owners, emphasizing understanding of local challenges and opportunities. Their Preferred Lender status enables faster SBA approval timelines compared to standard lenders. The availability of both federal SBA programs and state/proprietary products provides borrowers with genuine optionality based on their specific situation.

However, Pursuit's website lacks critical pricing transparency. While it mentions that SBA 504 loans carry "below-market, fixed rates" and SmartLoan is "fixed at 11.9%," it provides no interest rate ranges, APR disclosures, or fee structures for most products. Qualification criteria are vaguely stated ("positive history," "break-even or positive cash flow," "fair to good" credit), making it difficult for prospects to pre-qualify. The website does not disclose typical loan amounts, approval timelines, or specific eligibility requirements by program. This opacity requires applicants to contact the company for basic terms, limiting the ability to comparison shop.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: SBA Preferred Lender status enables faster underwriting and approval for SBA 504 and 7(a) products compared to standard lenders
SBA 504 loans offer terms up to 25 years with below-market fixed rates for owner-occupied commercial real estate
Wide product range (8+ programs) allows businesses to match financing to their specific need—working capital, real estate, equipment, refinancing
Local expertise and 70-year history serving NYC businesses across all five boroughs reduces friction for NYC entrepreneurs
Access to state programs like New York Forward Loan Fund 2 in addition to federal SBA products
Specialized programs for veterans with preferred rates and terms
Online application process and multiple funding use cases (working capital, debt refinance, asset acquisition)
cons: Interest rates and fees are not disclosed on the website for most products—only SmartLoan (11.9%) and vague mention of SBA 504 'below-market' rates; applicants must contact for quotes
Qualification criteria are vague ('fair to good' credit, 'positive cash flow')—no specific credit score minimums, debt service coverage ratio targets, or time-in-business requirements disclosed
No information on approval timelines, funding speed, or typical loan sizes by product, making it difficult to assess fit before application
Limited transparency on fees (origination fees, SBA guarantee fees, closing costs, prepayment penalties) for any product
Website does not clearly articulate which programs are best for specific business stages or industries, despite claiming versatility across 'nearly any business need'
best_for: NYC-based business owners seeking owner-occupied commercial real estate financing with 20+ year terms and fixed rates
Small businesses needing working capital or debt refinancing with flexible SBA 7(a) or proprietary line of credit options
Veteran-owned businesses in New York City looking for preferred lending terms and rates
Growing companies across multiple industries requiring $50K–$5.5M in capital with local lender support and understanding
diagnosis: Pursuit is best for established NYC business owners or entrepreneurs with solid credit and cash flow seeking SBA-backed financing for real estate, working capital, or growth—particularly those who value local expertise and relationship-based lending. The primary caveat is severe pricing and qualification opacity; applicants cannot comparison shop or self-qualify without direct contact, and the website reveals almost no interest rates, fees, or specific underwriting criteria beyond vague language.
services: SBA 504 Loans (up to $5.5M for owner-occupied commercial real estate)
SBA 7(a) Loans (up to $5M for working capital and general business financing)
Small Business Line of Credit (unlimited draws, flexible use)
Veteran Loan Programs (preferred rates and terms)
SBA 504 Refinance (for existing fixed asset debt)
New York Forward Loan Fund 2 (up to $150K, fixed rate)
Impact Loans (up to $500K, fixed rate for NYC businesses)
Online application and loan management platform
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
